# Cirth v0.3.0

Cirth v0.3.0 reduces the published theme surface to three maintained themes,
adds a VitePress documentation site, a changelog, and a package `exports`
map, and repositions the project against Pico CSS and Tailwind CSS in the
README.

## Highlights

- Reduced the inherited 20-accent theme set to three maintained themes —
  azure (default), jade, and slate — each a complete, self-contained theme
  rather than an accent swap on the default theme.
- Added a VitePress documentation site (`docs/`), styled with Cirth's own
  default build, with live examples for every component.
- Added a `package.json` `exports` map with named sub-paths for every
  published build (`@cirthcss/cirth/jade`, `@cirthcss/cirth/classless/scoped`,
  `@cirthcss/cirth/jade/classless/scoped`, ...), alongside the existing
  `dist/*.css` paths.
- Added `CHANGELOG.md`, replacing GitHub Releases as the primary source of
  truth for notable changes.
- Reworded the README status badge from "early stabilization" to "active
  development" and added a Comparison section against Pico CSS and Tailwind
  CSS.

## Build Surface

The primary generated files are unchanged:

- `dist/cirth.css` / `dist/cirth.min.css`
- `dist/cirth.classless.css` / `dist/cirth.classless.min.css`
- `dist/cirth.scoped.css` / `dist/cirth.scoped.min.css`
- `dist/cirth.classless.scoped.css` / `dist/cirth.classless.scoped.min.css`

Only three themes are now published, each in all four build shapes above:
azure (default, no suffix), `jade`, and `slate`.

## Migration Notes

- **Breaking:** the following theme builds are no longer generated or
  published: `amber`, `blue`, `cyan`, `fuchsia`, `green`, `grey`, `indigo`,
  `lime`, `orange`, `pink`, `pumpkin`, `purple`, `red`, `sand`, `violet`,
  `yellow`, `zinc`. If your project links one of these, switch to azure
  (default), `jade`, or `slate`, or override `--cirth-primary` and the rest
  of the primary color group yourself — see
  [Customization](https://github.com/cirthcss/cirth/blob/v0.3.0/docs/customization.md).
- The published npm tarball is significantly smaller as a result: ~3MB
  unpacked, down from ~19MB in v0.2.0.
- New: every build also has a named `exports` sub-path
  (`@cirthcss/cirth/jade`, `@cirthcss/cirth/classless/scoped`, ...). The
  existing `dist/*.css` import paths keep working unchanged.

## CDN

npm CDN URL:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@cirthcss/cirth@0.3.0/dist/cirth.min.css">
```
